title: Debug C# Console Applications - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of the Visual Studio debugger, breakpoints, stepping, watch windows, call stack, exception handling, try/catch/finally, and throw

questions.question: What is a breakpoint in debugging?

options.text: A line of code that causes a compilation error
A marker that tells the debugger to pause execution at a specific line
A statement that terminates the program
A checkpoint that saves the program state to disk

questions.explanation: A breakpoint is a marker set on a line of code that tells the debugger to pause (break) program execution at that point. When paused, you can inspect variable values, the call stack, and step through code line by line.

questions.question: What does 'Step Over' (F10) do in the Visual Studio debugger?

options.text: Skips the current line without executing it
Executes the current line and moves to the next, without entering any method calls
Jumps to the end of the current method
Moves to the previous line

questions.explanation: 'Step Over' executes the current line completely (including any method calls) and pauses at the next line. It does NOT step into any called methods — they execute as a single unit. Use 'Step Into' (F11) to enter method calls.

questions.question: Which block of code in a try/catch/finally structure ALWAYS executes?

options.text: try block
catch block
finally block
All blocks always execute

questions.explanation: The `finally` block always executes, whether or not an exception occurred. It runs after the try block (if no exception) or after the catch block (if an exception was caught). It's commonly used for cleanup like closing files or database connections.

questions.question: What is the correct syntax for catching a specific exception type in C#?

options.text: catch (Exception e) { }
catch FormatException (e) { }
except (FormatException e) { }
catch (FormatException ex) { }

questions.explanation: The syntax is `catch (ExceptionType variableName) { }`. For example, `catch (FormatException ex)` catches only FormatException. Option A catches ALL exceptions (since all inherit from Exception), which is less specific.

questions.question: What does the `throw` keyword do in C#?

options.text: Catches an exception
Raises (throws) an exception that can be caught by a catch block
Prints an error message to the console
Terminates the program immediately

questions.explanation: The `throw` keyword raises an exception. Example: `throw new ArgumentException("Invalid value");` creates and throws an exception. The runtime searches up the call stack for a matching catch block. If none is found, the program terminates.

questions.question: What does 'Step Into' (F11) do in the debugger?

options.text: Executes the current line and moves to the next line in the current method
If the current line contains a method call, enters that method and pauses at its first line
Exits the current method and returns to the caller
Runs the program until the next breakpoint

questions.explanation: 'Step Into' moves execution into the method being called on the current line, pausing at the first line inside that method. This lets you trace through method calls in detail. If the line has no method call, it behaves like Step Over.

questions.question: What is the call stack in debugging?

options.text: A list of all variables currently in scope
A record of the chain of method calls that led to the current execution point
A list of all breakpoints in the program
A queue of methods waiting to be executed

questions.explanation: The call stack shows the chain of method calls from the entry point to the current line of execution. It helps you understand how the program arrived at a particular point, which is crucial for debugging nested method calls.

questions.question: What exception is thrown when you try to convert "abc" to an integer using `int.Parse("abc")`?

options.text: ArgumentException
InvalidCastException
FormatException
OverflowException

questions.explanation: `int.Parse()` throws a `FormatException` when the string is not in the correct format for an integer. "abc" is not a valid integer representation. Use `int.TryParse()` to avoid exceptions when parsing might fail.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Watch window in Visual Studio?

options.text: To time how long methods take to execute
To monitor specific variables or expressions and see their values change during debugging
To watch for file changes in the project
To track the order in which methods are called

questions.explanation: The Watch window lets you add specific variables or expressions to monitor during debugging. As you step through code, the Watch window shows updated values for the watched items. You can also evaluate expressions on the fly.

questions.question: What does 'Step Out' (Shift+F11) do?

options.text: Exits the debugger completely
Runs the remaining lines in the current method and pauses at the calling method
Steps over the next line
Removes all breakpoints

questions.explanation: 'Step Out' completes execution of the current method and pauses at the line in the calling method after the method call returns. It's useful when you've stepped into a method and want to get back to the caller quickly.
